title: Igniting the Star Atlas Triad Tournament - A Super Phoenix Sports Live Esports Event
brief: This proposal seeks funding of the Ecosystem Fund limit (currently 13,576,392 ATLAS) for the production and broadcast of the Star Atlas Triad Tournament in June 2026, a first-ever combined competitive event spanning all three UE5 game modes: Racing Mode (Star Racer), Gun Mode (Star Shooter), Siege Mode (Star Fighter). Funding is to compensate the team only, it is not funding a prize pool.
description:
# Igniting the Star Atlas Triad Tournament - A Super Phoenix Sports Live Esports Event

* Author: Money, Lanzer, Njord
* Type: Ecosystem fund
* Date: April 2026

___

## 2. SUMMARY
This proposal seeks funding of the Ecosystem Fund limit (currently 13,576,392 ATLAS) for the production and broadcast of the Star Atlas Triad Tournament in June 2026, a first-ever combined competitive event spanning all three UE5 game modes: Racing Mode (Star Racer), Gun Mode (Star Shooter), Siege Mode (Star Fighter). Funding is to compensate the team only, it is not funding a prize pool.

Produced by the Super Phoenix Sports (SPS) team, this mega-tournament will be the most ambitious competitive Star Atlas event ever attempted and will be broadcast live across multiple platforms to a global audience. VODs available here: https://www.youtube.com/@superphoenixsports

Since Sep 2025, SPS has:

* Hosted six tournament events uniting DACs, rival factions, and external Web3 communities around competitive Star Atlas gameplay. 
* Two large-scale “Clash of Titans” tournaments featuring UE5 Gun Mode and Racing Mode ranked in the top 1% of Twitch and top 10% of YouTube livestreamers in Dec 2025 and Apr 2026. 
* Over 1,000 viewers tuned in across four platforms (Twitch, X Broadcast, Kick, YouTube Live). 
* More than 40 Star Atlas community players have competed. 
* SPS has paid out over $5,000 in USDC and $8,000 in ship NFTs in prize pools, almost entirely from its own resources.

By the end of May 2026, SPS will have hosted 10 or more tournament events.

This proposal asks the DAO to back the next chapter. A unified three-mode championship is the kind of marquee live event that elevates Star Atlas from a game with a competitive scene into a legitimate esport. It brings new eyes to the ecosystem, rewards active players, and demonstrates to the broader gaming world that Star Atlas has arrived.
___

## 3. MOTIVATION
The DAO's stated long-term vision is a player-owned, community-driven metaverse. Competitive live events are the most powerful tools available to build that community. This is how we get there.

Star Atlas has a live esports audience. The viewership of SPS tournaments proves it. The community has responded. The audience is real. The infrastructure exists.

Livestream history
* 621 live viewers across 4 events in April 2026
* 839 live viewers across 5 events in March 2026

___

## 4. SPECIFICATION
The Star Atlas Triad Tournament is a single-day championship event featuring three consecutive competitive brackets across Star Atlas's UE5 game modes:

* Star Racer - UE5 Racing Mode bracket
* Star Shooter - UE5 Gun Mode bracket
* Star Fighter - UE5 Siege Mode bracket

Each bracket operates as a standalone competition with its own prize allocation, seeding, and format. Combined scores or a points system across all three brackets will determine an overall Triad Champion.

The event will be broadcast live across Twitch, YouTube, and additional streaming platforms with professional commentary, overlays, and production quality consistent with SPS's prior events. A recorded highlight package will be distributed across Star Atlas and SPS social channels following the event.

Participation will be open to Star Atlas community members, including: DAC members, independent players, and invited Web3 community representatives.

Prize pool distribution will be drawn from outside fundraising.

___

## 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT
Star Atlas Players - Positively impacted. Competitive players gain a high-stakes, high-visibility event to participate in and rewarding prize pool.

POLIS Holders and DAO Members - Positively impacted. A successful broadcast event with strong viewership drives awareness of Star Atlas and increases the perceived value of the ecosystem. DAO funding is deployed toward community-visible output with measurable results.

Potential Negative Impact - The funding request represents a draw on Ecosystem Fund during a bear market.

Non-Competing Community Members - Viewers and community members who do not compete still benefit from the content and community energy generated by the event.
ATMTA and the Star Atlas DAO - Positively impacted. Third-party esports production at no cost to ATMTA amplifies the game's visibility at a critical moment in the UE5 launch cycle.

External Web3 and Gaming Communities - Positively impacted. SPS has a track record of bringing outside communities into Star Atlas events, expanding the game's reach beyond its existing player base. Examples include: SolGG, Decrypted.tax, Dream Test Labs

___

## 6. IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS
Upon approval of this PIP, the following steps will be executed in order:

* SPS confirms internal production team assignments and finalizes event format across all three brackets.
* SPS publishes official event announcements and broadcasts schedule across Star Atlas Discord, X, and SPS channels.
* Player registration opens. SPS manages registration, seeding, and bracket construction.
* SPS conducts a pre-event test broadcast to confirm technical setup across all streaming platforms.
* The Star Atlas Triad Tournament is hosted and broadcast live.

The DAO will continue to be featured throughout the tournament

Prize pool is distributed to winners following event completion.

SPS publishes a post-event report to the DAO, including: viewership numbers, participation data, and a full accounting of fund usage.

SPS is the responsible party for all production, broadcast, registration, and prize distribution. The event operates entirely on existing UE5 game modes.

Hosting Solution: Live broadcast across Twitch, X, and additional platforms. Event coordination managed via SPS internal infrastructure and existing community channels.

Potential Challenges: 

* Scheduling. Player availability and scheduling across time zones will be managed through advance registration and clearly published bracket timelines. Low registration will be addressed through active community outreach beginning immediately upon PIP approval using previously established best practices by the SPS Team.
* State of the Game Modes. Different game modes are at various degrees of completion. There are known bugs that make hosting a tournament difficult (examples: invincibility bug, game crashing, 5-minute game start delay bug, and spectator camera bugs). These bugs have caused previous tournaments to be postponed. As of Apr 12, 2026: Gun Mode is stable enough to host weekly tournaments, Racing Mode is hostable but not entirely stable, and Siege Mode has multiple stability issues that may not be fixed before the Triad Tournament is scheduled to be held. SPS Team and the Star Atlas development team are in constant communication about feature requests to improve the state of the game. Should any one game mode prove too unstable to host a quality tournament event (examples: if spectator camera doesn’t work or lobby won’t start the match), SPS Team will need to either delay the tournament or rescope the tournament as a compromise. It will be the responsibility of the SPS Team to determine this. Examples of previous mitigation techniques: creating redundant video tracks to stream, screenshotting match results, players share screens with SPS Referee, restarting matches where fair play or game integrity is broken.

A return on investment (ROI) is offered to give back to the DAO. See Section 8.

___

## 7. TIMELINE
Projected Timeline

* PIP Approval: Late-May 2026 
* Event Announcement and Registration Open: Within 7 days of PIP vote concluding
* Pre-Event Test Broadcast: One week prior to event
* Star Atlas Triad Tournament Live Event: End of June 2026. Per potential challenges noted in paragraph 6(f)(ii). 
* Start within 1 month of a fix to Siege Mode being made, note: Per PIP-23 “Refresh of Ecosystem Fund”, a PIP may not extend for more than 1 year. If a delay is needed, then the event has up to 6 months to complete.
* Prize Distribution: Within 7 days of event completion 
* Post-Event DAO Report: Within 14 days of event completion
* Payment Schedule: Please see below proposed milestone payments

Milestones

* Conclude PIP Vote (25% payment): If PIP concludes with a majority YES vote, then initial payment is requested no later than 5 days after PIP vote concludes.
* Complete Tournament Event (50% payment): Not later than 1 year from date PIP vote concludes.
* Publish Results Report (25% payment): No later than 14 days after the event concludes

___

## 8. COST & RESOURCE ALLOCATION
One-Time Costs: 13,576,392 ATLAS

* Operations and Broadcast - ATLAS will be allocated to personnel of SPS Team and required infrastructure (multi-platform streaming requirements and technical stack). The funded amount compensates approximately 20 staff members.

* Funding is to compensate the team only, it is not funding a prize pool.

Recurring Cost: 0 ATLAS

* N/A

Total Requested: Ecosystem Fund limit, 13,576,392 ATLAS

Source of Funding: Star Atlas DAO Ecosystem Fund, PIP-23

* Alternative Funding: SPS has self-funded over $13K USDC in prior prize pools, personnel, and production costs across six events. This proposal represents the first DAO funding request made by SPS. Sponsorship outreach to external Web3 partners is ongoing and may supplement future events.
* Ongoing Costs: N/A. This proposal covers a single event. Future events will be subject to separate proposals or alternative funding arrangements.

Return on Investment (ROI). 

* Even during the current bear market, esports viewership is strong. SPS has activated the larger web3 community through not only player participation, but also sponsorships, advertisements, and bracket sweepstakes. The SPS Team wants the DAO to share in its success.
* SPS will give 1% of revenue earned from the Triad Tournament back to the DAO.

___

## 9. ADDITIONAL DETAILS
* Super Phoenix Sports is powered by Super Phoenix DAO (SPD), a longstanding diamond hands member of the Star Atlas community and owner of the Super Phoenix (https://superphoenixdao.com/landing). This conflict of interest is mitigated through SPS being a separately registered company and transparent post-event reporting.
* Lanzer is currently the Director of the SPS Team. He also serves on the DAO Council (SAC-3) and is co-author of this PIP. Lanzer will recuse himself from all necessary activities where an impartial decision is needed as council-member (milestone payment release vote, SAC-3 vote for PIP to proceed to Foundation review).
* Morango is currently on the SPS Team. She also serves on the DAO Council (SAC-3). She will recuse herself from all necessary activities where an impartial decision is needed as council-member (milestone payment release vote, SAC-3 vote for PIP to proceed to Foundation review).

Earlier Versions: This proposal has not been previously submitted or rejected. Separate SPS Tournament Events have been previously funded under PIP-22 “Iris Bounty: The Arena” events. Cost to produce tournaments greatly exceeds the Iris Bounty Arena max funding limit of ~$250, and historical revenue has been less than $1K per month, which resulted in this PIP.

Reference: Past SPS broadcast available for community review at https://www.youtube.com/@superphoenixsports

Power to the People.
